topic: The composer audit: 6 things to fix before you pitch
hook: Before you send your next sync pitch, run this audit.

slides.eyebrow: Education · The composer audit: 6 things to fix before you pitch
slides.headline: Before you send your next sync pitch, run this audit.
Most composers who fail at the brief stage don't fail on music quality.
slides.lede: They fail on one of these 6 things. ↓

slides.headline:
1/ RIGHTS CHAIN DOCUMENTATION
Can you confirm in writing who owns the master and who controls publishing?
slides.lede:
Not "probably me." Confirmed and documented.
If not: fix this before anything else.

One uncleared sample in your best track = that track is unplaceable.
If you're unsure: assume it's not cleared.

slides.headline:
3/ FILE QUALITY
Do you have broadcast-spec WAV files for every track you're pitching?
slides.lede:
Not 320k MP3. Not a Spotify link.
Ready to send today.

slides.headline:
4/ STEMS
When a supervisor asks "can I get stems?" — do you have them?
slides.lede:
Not "I'll re-export from the session."
Organised, named, cloud-accessible within 10 minutes.

slides.headline:
5/ METADATA
Is your ISRC embedded in the file?
slides.lede:
Are BPM, key, and mood tags complete?
Is your PRO registration current and reflected in the metadata?

slides.headline:
6/ RESPONSE TIME
Can you respond to a brief within 24 hours?
slides.lede:
Not "I'll check when I see it."
An active notification. A clear process. A fast reply. · Supervisors give the placement to whoever responds first with the right track.

slides.headline: Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede: Not the best track. The right track, first. · Audit now. Fix the gaps before the brief lands.
list_items: Not the best track. The right track, first.
Audit now. Fix the gaps before the brief lands.

slides.headline: Open briefs. Every Tuesday.
slides.lede: Composers: apply once, get matched for life. Supervisors: shortlist in 48h.
